title: Laminated panoramic roof with improved aesthetics
abstract: The laminated glass panoramic roof is a popular option offered on a growing number of automobiles. Much of its appeal comes from the open airy feel that it imparts upon the cabin and the sleek modern appearance that it gives to the vehicle. However, as the quantity, brightness and the size of instrument and information displays has increased reflected light, from the panoramic roof, has become more and more of a concern. The invention, through the application of glass surface treatments in combination with glass compositions and plastic interlayers, significantly reduces these reflections improving the interior aesthetic of the laminate.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A panoramic vehicle roof laminate comprising: a first glass layer having first and second oppositely disposed major faces with the first major face of said first glass layer serving as an exterior surface of the vehicle; a second glass layer, having first and second oppositely disposed major faces, the second major face of the second glass layer serving as an interior surface of the vehicle; at least one plastic bonding layer having first and second oppositely disposed major faces; wherein the at least one plastic bonding layer is positioned between and affixing opposite major surfaces of adjacent glass layers to each other; wherein the total visible light transmittance of the laminate is less than about 5%; wherein the interior surface of the second glass layer has been treated to reduce the reflection of light. 2. The laminate of claim 1, wherein the total visible light reflectance is less than about 1%., preferably less than about 0.5%. 3. The laminate of claim 1, wherein the visible light transmittance of said at least one bonding layer is less than about 50%. 4. The laminate of claim 1, wherein the treatment is an anti-reflection coating. 5. The laminate of claim 1, wherein the treatment is an anti -glare coating. 6. The laminate of claim 1, wherein the treatment is an anti -reflection surface treatment. 7. The laminate of claim 1, wherein the treatment is an anti -glare surface treatment. 8. The laminate of claim 1, further comprising an infrared reflecting coating on one of the glass surfaces. 9. The laminate of claim 1, further comprising an infrared reflecting film. 10. The laminate of claim 1, further comprising an infrared absorbing layer. 11. A vehicle comprising the vehicle roof laminate of claim 1.
